aiTitle: Debug NFS export path failures in devbox setup

content.text:
Now the real blocker is clear. The `devbox` stack is missing both required files — `config.json` and `cloud-init.yaml` — and the Makefile points at the wrong stack name (`localdev`) and uses a non-existent goloo command (`delete` instead of `destroy`).

Let me fix the Makefile now (clearly correct), and read `VM-SHARED-MOUNTS.md` since the cloud-init scripts come from it.

content.content:
1	# VM Shared Mounts: Host Filesystem Shadow via NFS
2	
3	Disclaimer: This works for me — that's the entire guarantee. Built with AI in the loop, so check your own biases before you love it or hate it on principle. Use at your own risk, fork freely, and don't @ me when it explodes. (But do drop me a note if it helps — pay it forward.)
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Overview
8	
9	This document describes how to create a persistent Linux VM (QEMU/KVM) that presents a **shadow view of the host filesystem** with configurable per-path read/write permissions:
10	
11	- The entire host filesystem is visible inside the VM as **read-only**
12	- Specific directories you designate are **read-write**, with writes going directly to the host
13	- Everything is driven by a single config file shared between host and VM
14	- Mounts can be set up and torn down on demand from the command line
15	
16	The mechanism is:
17	1. **NFS** exports the host filesystem — once as read-only (catch-all), and once per RW path (explicitly listed)
18	2. Inside the VM, the RO root is mounted first, then RW NFS mounts are **bind-mounted** over their corresponding paths in the shadow tree, masking the RO view
19	
20	---
21	
22	## Architecture
23	
24	```
25	Host OS (Ubuntu)
26	├── NFS server
27	│   ├── / exported RO (catch-all shadow)
28	│   ├── /localdev exported RW
29	│   └── /home/user/projects exported RW
30	└── /etc/host-shadow-paths  ← single source of truth
31	
32	VM (QEMU/KVM, Ubuntu)
33	├── /mnt/host/              ← RO shadow of entire host
34	│   ├── /mnt/host/etc       ← read-only
35	│   ├── /mnt/host/localdev  ← read-write (bind mount over RO)
36	│   └── /mnt/host/home/user/projects  ← read-write (bind mount over RO)
37	├── /mnt/rw/                ← staging area for RW NFS mounts
38	├── /etc/host-shadow-paths  ← synced from host
39	└── /usr/local/bin/shadow-{mount,umount,sync}
40	```
41	
42	---
43	
44	## Requirements
45	
46	- Ubuntu 22.04 or 24.04 on both host and VM
47	- QEMU/KVM with the VM on the default NAT network (`10.0.2.0/24`)
48	- The host's address from inside the VM is `10.0.2.2` (QEMU default)
49	- Root/sudo on both host and VM
50	
51	---
52	
53	## Part 1: Host Setup
54	
55	### 1.1 Install Packages
56	
57	```bash
58	sudo apt update
59	sudo apt install -y nfs-kernel-server
60	```
61	
62	### 1.2 Create the Path List
63	
64	This file is the **single source of truth** for which paths are read-write. Both the host exports and the VM mount script are generated from it.
65	
66	```bash
67	sudo tee /etc/host-shadow-paths << 'EOF'
68	/localdev
69	/home/greg/projects
70	EOF
71	```
72	
73	Add or remove lines here whenever your RW path set changes. One absolute path per line. Blank lines and lines beginning with `#` are ignored.
74	
75	### 1.3 Generate /etc/exports
76	
77	Run this script whenever `/etc/host-shadow-paths` changes:
78	
79	```bash
80	sudo tee /usr/local/bin/shadow-export-gen << 'EOF'
81	#!/bin/bash
82	set -e
83	
84	PATHS_FILE=/etc/host-shadow-paths
85	EXPORTS_FILE=/etc/exports
86	VM_NETWORK="10.0.2.0/24"
87	
88	# Write the RO catch-all first
89	cat > $EXPORTS_FILE << EXPORTS
90	# Auto-generated by shadow-export-gen — do not edit manually
91	# RO catch-all: entire host tree visible read-only
92	/  ${VM_NETWORK}(ro,sync,fsid=0,crossmnt,no_subtree_check)
93	EXPORTS
94	
95	# Write one RW export per path
96	while IFS= read -r path || [[ -n "$path" ]]; do
97	    # Skip blank lines and comments
98	    [[ -z "$path" || "$path" =~ ^# ]] && continue
99	    echo "${path}  ${VM_NETWORK}(rw,sync,no_subtree_check,no_root_squash)" >> $EXPORTS_FILE
100	done < $PATHS_FILE
101	
102	exportfs -ra
103	echo "Exports updated:"
104	exportfs -v
105	EOF
106	
107	sudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/shadow-export-gen
108	```
109	
110	Run it now to generate the initial exports:
111	
112	```bash
113	sudo shadow-export-gen
114	```
115	
116	Verify:
117	
118	```bash
119	sudo exportfs -v
120	```
121	
122	Expected output (paths will vary):
123	
124	```
125	/                 10.0.2.0/24(ro,sync,wdelay,hide,crossmnt,no_subtree_check,fsid=0,...)
126	/localdev         10.0.2.0/24(rw,sync,wdelay,hide,no_subtree_check,no_root_squash,...)
127	/home/greg/projects
128	                  10.0.2.0/24(rw,sync,wdelay,hide,no_subtree_check,no_root_squash,...)
129	```
130	
131	### 1.4 Enable and Start NFS
132	
133	```bash
134	sudo systemctl enable --now nfs-kernel-server
135	sudo systemctl status nfs-kernel-server
136	```
137	
138	### 1.5 Firewall (if ufw is active)
139	
140	QEMU's default NAT network is on `10.0.2.0/24`. Allow NFS from it:
141	
142	```bash
143	sudo ufw allow from 10.0.2.0/24 to any port nfs
144	sudo ufw allow from 10.0.2.0/24 to any port 111
145	sudo ufw reload
146	```
147	
148	---
149	
150	## Part 2: VM Setup
151	
152	These steps run **inside the VM**.
153	
154	### 2.1 Install Packages
155	
156	```bash
157	sudo apt update
158	sudo apt install -y nfs-common
159	```
160	
161	### 2.2 Create Mount Directories
162	
163	```bash
164	sudo mkdir -p /mnt/host
165	sudo mkdir -p /mnt/rw
166	```
167	
168	### 2.3 Install the Path List
169	
170	Copy from the host (run from inside the VM):
171	
172	```bash
173	sudo scp user@10.0.2.2:/etc/host-shadow-paths /etc/host-shadow-paths
174	```
175	
176	Or create it manually — it must match the host's `/etc/host-shadow-paths` exactly:
177	
178	```bash
179	sudo tee /etc/host-shadow-paths << 'EOF'
180	/localdev
181	/home/greg/projects
182	EOF
183	```
184	
185	### 2.4 Install the Mount Script
186	
187	```bash
188	sudo tee /usr/local/bin/shadow-mount << 'EOF'
189	#!/bin/bash
190	set -e
191	
192	HOST=10.0.2.2
193	SHADOW_ROOT=/mnt/host
194	RW_STAGING=/mnt/rw
195	PATHS_FILE=/etc/host-shadow-paths
196	
197	if mountpoint -q $SHADOW_ROOT; then
198	    echo "Shadow already mounted at $SHADOW_ROOT"
199	    exit 0
200	fi
201	
202	echo "Mounting RO shadow of host at $SHADOW_ROOT..."
203	mount -t nfs4 -o ro,soft,timeo=30 $HOST:/ $SHADOW_ROOT
204	
205	while IFS= read -r path || [[ -n "$path" ]]; do
206	    [[ -z "$path" || "$path" =~ ^# ]] && continue
207	
208	    staging=$RW_STAGING$path
209	    target=$SHADOW_ROOT$path
210	
211	    echo "  Overlaying RW: $path"
212	    mkdir -p "$staging" "$target"
213	    mount -t nfs4 -o rw,soft,timeo=30 $HOST:$path $staging
214	    mount --bind $staging $target
215	done < $PATHS_FILE
216	
217	echo "Shadow mounted. RO root at $SHADOW_ROOT, RW paths overlaid."
218	EOF
219	
220	sudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/shadow-mount
221	```
222	
223	### 2.5 Install the Unmount Script
224	
225	```bash
226	sudo tee /usr/local/bin/shadow-umount << 'EOF'
227	#!/bin/bash
228	set -e
229	
230	SHADOW_ROOT=/mnt/host
231	RW_STAGING=/mnt/rw
232	PATHS_FILE=/etc/host-shadow-paths
233	
234	if ! mountpoint -q $SHADOW_ROOT; then
235	    echo "Shadow is not mounted."
236	    exit 0
237	fi
238	
239	echo "Unmounting RW overlays..."
240	
241	# Collect paths in reverse order so nested paths unmount before parents
242	mapfile -t paths < <(grep -v '^\s*#' $PATHS_FILE | grep -v '^\s*$' | tac)
243	
244	for path in "${paths[@]}"; do
245	    target=$SHADOW_ROOT$path
246	    staging=$RW_STAGING$path
247	
248	    if mountpoint -q "$target" 2>/dev/null; then
249	        echo "  Unmounting bind: $target"
250	        umount "$target"
251	    fi
252	
253	    if mountpoint -q "$staging" 2>/dev/null; then
254	        echo "  Unmounting RW NFS: $staging"
255	        umount "$staging"
256	    fi
257	done
258	
259	echo "Unmounting RO root..."
260	umount $SHADOW_ROOT
261	
262	echo "Shadow unmounted."
263	EOF
264	
265	sudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/shadow-umount
266	```
267	
268	### 2.6 Install the Sync Script
269	
270	Keeps the VM's path list in sync with the host when you add or remove RW paths:
271	
272	```bash
273	sudo tee /usr/local/bin/shadow-sync << 'EOF'
274	#!/bin/bash
275	set -e
276	
277	HOST=10.0.2.2
278	PATHS_FILE=/etc/host-shadow-paths
279	
280	echo "Syncing path list from host..."
281	scp user@$HOST:/etc/host-shadow-paths $PATHS_FILE
282	echo "Done. Current RW paths:"
283	grep -v '^\s*#' $PATHS_FILE | grep -v '^\s*$'
284	EOF
285

file.content:
# VM Shared Mounts: Host Filesystem Shadow via NFS

Disclaimer: This works for me — that's the entire guarantee. Built with AI in the loop, so check your own biases before you love it or hate it on principle. Use at your own risk, fork freely, and don't @ me when it explodes. (But do drop me a note if it helps — pay it forward.)

---

## Overview

This document describes how to create a persistent Linux VM (QEMU/KVM) that presents a **shadow view of the host filesystem** with configurable per-path read/write permissions:

- The entire host filesystem is visible inside the VM as **read-only**
- Specific directories you designate are **read-write**, with writes going directly to the host
- Everything is driven by a single config file shared between host and VM
- Mounts can be set up and torn down on demand from the command line

The mechanism is:
1. **NFS** exports the host filesystem — once as read-only (catch-all), and once per RW path (explicitly listed)
2. Inside the VM, the RO root is mounted first, then RW NFS mounts are **bind-mounted** over their corresponding paths in the shadow tree, masking the RO view

---

## Architecture

```
Host OS (Ubuntu)
├── NFS server
│   ├── / exported RO (catch-all shadow)
│   ├── /localdev exported RW
│   └── /home/user/projects exported RW
└── /etc/host-shadow-paths  ← single source of truth

VM (QEMU/KVM, Ubuntu)
├── /mnt/host/              ← RO shadow of entire host
│   ├── /mnt/host/etc       ← read-only
│   ├── /mnt/host/localdev  ← read-write (bind mount over RO)
│   └── /mnt/host/home/user/projects  ← read-write (bind mount over RO)
├── /mnt/rw/                ← staging area for RW NFS mounts
├── /etc/host-shadow-paths  ← synced from host
└── /usr/local/bin/shadow-{mount,umount,sync}
```

---

## Requirements

- Ubuntu 22.04 or 24.04 on both host and VM
- QEMU/KVM with the VM on the default NAT network (`10.0.2.0/24`)
- The host's address from inside the VM is `10.0.2.2` (QEMU default)
- Root/sudo on both host and VM

---

## Part 1: Host Setup

### 1.1 Install Packages

```bash
sudo apt update
sudo apt install -y nfs-kernel-server
```

### 1.2 Create the Path List

This file is the **single source of truth** for which paths are read-write. Both the host exports and the VM mount script are generated from it.

```bash
sudo tee /etc/host-shadow-paths << 'EOF'
/localdev
/home/greg/projects
EOF
```

Add or remove lines here whenever your RW path set changes. One absolute path per line. Blank lines and lines beginning with `#` are ignored.

### 1.3 Generate /etc/exports

Run this script whenever `/etc/host-shadow-paths` changes:

```bash
sudo tee /usr/local/bin/shadow-export-gen << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
set -e

PATHS_FILE=/etc/host-shadow-paths
EXPORTS_FILE=/etc/exports
VM_NETWORK="10.0.2.0/24"

# Write the RO catch-all first
cat > $EXPORTS_FILE << EXPORTS
# Auto-generated by shadow-export-gen — do not edit manually
# RO catch-all: entire host tree visible read-only
/  ${VM_NETWORK}(ro,sync,fsid=0,crossmnt,no_subtree_check)
EXPORTS

# Write one RW export per path
while IFS= read -r path || [[ -n "$path" ]]; do
    # Skip blank lines and comments
    [[ -z "$path" || "$path" =~ ^# ]] && continue
    echo "${path}  ${VM_NETWORK}(rw,sync,no_subtree_check,no_root_squash)" >> $EXPORTS_FILE
done < $PATHS_FILE

exportfs -ra
echo "Exports updated:"
exportfs -v
EOF

sudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/shadow-export-gen
```

Run it now to generate the initial exports:

```bash
sudo shadow-export-gen
```

Verify:

```bash
sudo exportfs -v
```

Expected output (paths will vary):

```
/                 10.0.2.0/24(ro,sync,wdelay,hide,crossmnt,no_subtree_check,fsid=0,...)
/localdev         10.0.2.0/24(rw,sync,wdelay,hide,no_subtree_check,no_root_squash,...)
/home/greg/projects
                  10.0.2.0/24(rw,sync,wdelay,hide,no_subtree_check,no_root_squash,...)
```

### 1.4 Enable and Start NFS

```bash
sudo systemctl enable --now nfs-kernel-server
sudo systemctl status nfs-kernel-server
```

### 1.5 Firewall (if ufw is active)

QEMU's default NAT network is on `10.0.2.0/24`. Allow NFS from it:

```bash
sudo ufw allow from 10.0.2.0/24 to any port nfs
sudo ufw allow from 10.0.2.0/24 to any port 111
sudo ufw reload
```

---

## Part 2: VM Setup

These steps run **inside the VM**.

### 2.1 Install Packages

```bash
sudo apt update
sudo apt install -y nfs-common
```

### 2.2 Create Mount Directories

```bash
sudo mkdir -p /mnt/host
sudo mkdir -p /mnt/rw
```

### 2.3 Install the Path List

Copy from the host (run from inside the VM):

```bash
sudo scp user@10.0.2.2:/etc/host-shadow-paths /etc/host-shadow-paths
```

Or create it manually — it must match the host's `/etc/host-shadow-paths` exactly:

```bash
sudo tee /etc/host-shadow-paths << 'EOF'
/localdev
/home/greg/projects
EOF
```

### 2.4 Install the Mount Script

```bash
sudo tee /usr/local/bin/shadow-mount << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
set -e

HOST=10.0.2.2
SHADOW_ROOT=/mnt/host
RW_STAGING=/mnt/rw
PATHS_FILE=/etc/host-shadow-paths

if mountpoint -q $SHADOW_ROOT; then
    echo "Shadow already mounted at $SHADOW_ROOT"
    exit 0
fi

echo "Mounting RO shadow of host at $SHADOW_ROOT..."
mount -t nfs4 -o ro,soft,timeo=30 $HOST:/ $SHADOW_ROOT

while IFS= read -r path || [[ -n "$path" ]]; do
    [[ -z "$path" || "$path" =~ ^# ]] && continue

    staging=$RW_STAGING$path
    target=$SHADOW_ROOT$path

    echo "  Overlaying RW: $path"
    mkdir -p "$staging" "$target"
    mount -t nfs4 -o rw,soft,timeo=30 $HOST:$path $staging
    mount --bind $staging $target
done < $PATHS_FILE

echo "Shadow mounted. RO root at $SHADOW_ROOT, RW paths overlaid."
EOF

sudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/shadow-mount
```

### 2.5 Install the Unmount Script

```bash
sudo tee /usr/local/bin/shadow-umount << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
set -e

SHADOW_ROOT=/mnt/host
RW_STAGING=/mnt/rw
PATHS_FILE=/etc/host-shadow-paths

if ! mountpoint -q $SHADOW_ROOT; then
    echo "Shadow is not mounted."
    exit 0
fi

echo "Unmounting RW overlays..."

# Collect paths in reverse order so nested paths unmount before parents
mapfile -t paths < <(grep -v '^\s*#' $PATHS_FILE | grep -v '^\s*$' | tac)

for path in "${paths[@]}"; do
    target=$SHADOW_ROOT$path
    staging=$RW_STAGING$path

    if mountpoint -q "$target" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "  Unmounting bind: $target"
        umount "$target"
    fi

    if mountpoint -q "$staging" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "  Unmounting RW NFS: $staging"
        umount "$staging"
    fi
done

echo "Unmounting RO root..."
umount $SHADOW_ROOT

echo "Shadow unmounted."
EOF

sudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/shadow-umount
```

### 2.6 Install the Sync Script

Keeps the VM's path list in sync with the host when you add or remove RW paths:

```bash
sudo tee /usr/local/bin/shadow-sync << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
set -e

HOST=10.0.2.2
PATHS_FILE=/etc/host-shadow-paths

echo "Syncing path list from host..."
scp user@$HOST:/etc/host-shadow-paths $PATHS_FILE
echo "Done. Current RW paths:"
grep -v '^\s*#' $PATHS_FILE | grep -v '^\s*$'
EOF

sudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/shadow-sync
```

Replace `user` with your actual host username.

---

## Part 3: Usage

### Mount the Shadow

```bash
sudo shadow-mount
```

### Verify

```bash
# List what is mounted
mount | grep '/mnt/host'

# Try reading a RO path
ls /mnt/host/etc

# Try writing to a RO path — should fail
touch /mnt/host/etc/test 2>&1
# touch: cannot touch '/mnt/host/etc/test': Read-only file system

# Write to a RW path — should succeed
touch /mnt/host/localdev/test-file
ls /mnt/host/localdev/test-file
rm /mnt/host/localdev/test-file
```

### Run Claude Code Against a Host Project

```bash
sudo shadow-mount
cd /mnt/host/localdev/my-project
claude
```

### Unmount the Shadow

```bash
sudo shadow-umount
```

---

## Part 4: Adding a New RW Path

### On the host

```bash
# 1. Add the path to the source of truth
echo '/home/greg/new-project' | sudo tee -a /etc/host-shadow-paths

# 2. Regenerate exports
sudo shadow-export-gen
```

### In the VM

```bash
# 3. Sync the path list from host
sudo shadow-sync

# 4. If shadow is already mounted, add the new path live
sudo mkdir -p /mnt/rw/home/greg/new-project /mnt/host/home/greg/new-project
sudo mount -t nfs4 -o rw 10.0.2.2:/home/greg/new-project /mnt/rw/home/greg/new-project
sudo mount --bind /mnt/rw/home/greg/new-project /mnt/host/home/greg/new-project

# Or simply unmount and remount to pick up all changes
sudo shadow-umount && sudo shadow-mount
```

---

## Part 5: Auto-Mount on VM Boot (Optional)

If you want the shadow mounted automatically when the VM starts:

```bash
sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/host-shadow.service << 'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=Host filesystem shadow mount
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=oneshot
RemainAfterExit=yes
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/shadow-mount
ExecStop=/usr/local/bin/shadow-umount

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable host-shadow.service
```

---

## Part 6: Troubleshooting

### NFS mount hangs or times out

Check the NFS server is running on the host:

```bash
# On host
sudo systemctl status nfs-kernel-server
sudo exportfs -v
```

Check connectivity from the VM:

```bash
# In VM
showmount -e 10.0.2.2
```

### "Permission denied" when mounting RW path

The path is not listed in the host's `/etc/exports` as a separate RW export. The RO catch-all matches first and the server rejects the `rw` mount request.

Fix: add the path to `/etc/host-shadow-paths` on the host, run `sudo shadow-export-gen`, then re-sync in the VM.

```bash
# Host
echo '/missing/path' | sudo tee -a /etc/host-shadow-paths
sudo shadow-export-gen

# VM
sudo shadow-sync
sudo shadow-umount && sudo shadow-mount
```

### "Already mounted" or stale mounts after VM crash

```bash
# Force unmount everything under /mnt/host
sudo umount -R /mnt/host
sudo umount -R /mnt/rw
```

### Writes fail even on a designated RW path

Confirm the bind mount is in place:

```bash
mount | grep '/mnt/host/localdev'
# Should show two entries: the NFS rw mount at /mnt/rw/localdev
# and the bind mount at /mnt/host/localdev
```

If only the RO NFS root appears, the bind mount step failed. Re-run `shadow-mount` or apply the path live as shown in Part 4.

### Check NFS version in use

```bash
# In VM
nfsstat -m | grep vers
```

All mounts should show `vers=4`. If you see `vers=3`, the NFS server may not have NFSv4 enabled:

```bash
# On host
sudo grep -i nfsvers /etc/default/nfs-kernel-server
# Ensure RPCNFSDARGS does not restrict to v3 only
```

---

## Reference: File Locations

| File | Host/VM | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `/etc/host-shadow-paths` | Both (must match) | Canonical list of RW paths |
| `/etc/exports` | Host | Auto-generated NFS exports |
| `/usr/local/bin/shadow-export-gen` | Host | Regenerates `/etc/exports` from path list |
| `/usr/local/bin/shadow-mount` | VM | Mounts the RO shadow + RW overlays |
| `/usr/local/bin/shadow-umount` | VM | Tears down all mounts cleanly |
| `/usr/local/bin/shadow-sync` | VM | Pulls updated path list from host |
| `/mnt/host` | VM | Shadow root — browse host filesystem here |
| `/mnt/rw` | VM | Staging area for RW NFS mounts (internal use) |

content.text:
Schema confirmed against the binary (`required 'vm' section`, `github_username` validation). REQUIREMENTS.md's format is correct. Now I'll create the two missing files.

First, `config.json` (minimal — NFS shadow handles host access, so no bind `mounts`):

content.text: Now the big one — `cloud-init.yaml`, generated from the REQUIREMENTS.md spec with the shadow scripts from VM-SHARED-MOUNTS.md. I'm adapting the host IP to be derived from the VM's default gateway at runtime (robust for the Multipass bridge, which doesn't use the QEMU `10.0.2.2` default), and setting the three RW paths to match the host:

input.content:
#cloud-config
# devbox local development VM — provisioned by goloo + Multipass.
# Generated from REQUIREMENTS.md (§4) and VM-SHARED-MOUNTS.md.
#
# Host-IP note: the shadow scripts derive the NFS server address from the VM's
# default-route gateway at runtime (= the Multipass bridge / host address).
# This avoids hard-coding the QEMU 10.0.2.2 default, which Multipass on Linux
# does not use.

users:
  - name: ubuntu
    sudo: ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL
    shell: /bin/bash
    ssh_authorized_keys:
      - ${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY}

package_update: true
package_upgrade: true

packages:
  # Core build tools
  - build-essential
  - cmake
  - make
  - pkg-config

  # Shell utilities
  - curl
  - wget
  - git
  - unzip
  - zip
  - file
  - xxd
  - jq
  - tree
  - rsync
  - tmux
  - mg
  - zoxide
  - keychain
  - direnv
  - stow
  - zstd
  - squashfs-tools

  # Media/document processing
  - ffmpeg
  - qpdf
  - imagemagick
  - pandoc

  # WeasyPrint runtime dependencies (for pandoc PDF output)
  - libpango-1.0-0
  - libpangoft2-1.0-0
  - libharfbuzz0b
  - libcairo2
  - libgdk-pixbuf-2.0-0
  - libffi8
  - shared-mime-info
  - fonts-dejavu
  - fonts-liberation

  # Python runtime
  - python3
  - python3-pip
  - python3-venv
  - python3-dev

  # USB / hardware access
  - libpcap-dev
  - libusb-1.0-0
  - libusb-1.0-0-dev
  - usbutils
  - udev

  # Podman and rootless container support
  - podman
  - uidmap
  - fuse-overlayfs
  - slirp4netns

  # NFS client (for host shadow filesystem)
  - nfs-common

  # CA certificates and HTTPS tooling
  - ca-certificates
  - gnupg
  - lsb-release
  - software-properties-common
  - apt-transport-https

  # Search / navigation tools
  - ripgrep
  - fd-find
  - fzf

write_files:
  # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Podman rootless-friendly defaults (matches the Containerfile)
  # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  - path: /etc/containers/containers.conf
    permissions: '0644'
    content: |
      [containers]
      netns="host"
      userns="host"
      ipcns="host"
      utsns="host"
      cgroupns="host"
      cgroups="disabled"
      devices=["/dev/null"]

      [engine]
      cgroup_manager="cgroupfs"
      events_logger="file"
      runtime="crun"

  # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  # NFS shadow mount: RO root of host + RW overlays per listed path
  # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  - path: /usr/local/bin/shadow-mount
    permissions: '0755'
    content: |
      #!/bin/bash
      set -e

      # Host = the VM's default gateway (Multipass bridge address).
      HOST=$(ip route show default 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $3; exit}')
      SHADOW_ROOT=/mnt/host
      RW_STAGING=/mnt/rw
      PATHS_FILE=/etc/host-shadow-paths

      if [ -z "$HOST" ]; then
          echo "Could not determine host IP from default route." >&2
          exit 1
      fi

      if mountpoint -q $SHADOW_ROOT; then
          echo "Shadow already mounted at $SHADOW_ROOT"
          exit 0
      fi

      echo "Mounting RO shadow of host ($HOST) at $SHADOW_ROOT..."
      mount -t nfs4 -o ro,soft,timeo=30 $HOST:/ $SHADOW_ROOT

      while IFS= read -r path || [[ -n "$path" ]]; do
          [[ -z "$path" || "$path" =~ ^# ]] && continue

          staging=$RW_STAGING$path
          target=$SHADOW_ROOT$path

          echo "  Overlaying RW: $path"
          mkdir -p "$staging" "$target"
          mount -t nfs4 -o rw,soft,timeo=30 $HOST:$path $staging
          mount --bind $staging $target
      done < $PATHS_FILE

      echo "Shadow mounted. RO root at $SHADOW_ROOT, RW paths overlaid."

  - path: /usr/local/bin/shadow-umount
    permissions: '0755'
    content: |
      #!/bin/bash
      set -e

      SHADOW_ROOT=/mnt/host
      RW_STAGING=/mnt/rw
      PATHS_FILE=/etc/host-shadow-paths

      if ! mountpoint -q $SHADOW_ROOT; then
          echo "Shadow is not mounted."
          exit 0
      fi

      echo "Unmounting RW overlays..."

      # Reverse order so nested paths unmount before parents
      mapfile -t paths < <(grep -v '^\s*#' $PATHS_FILE | grep -v '^\s*$' | tac)

      for path in "${paths[@]}"; do
          target=$SHADOW_ROOT$path
          staging=$RW_STAGING$path

          if mountpoint -q "$target" 2>/dev/null; then
              echo "  Unmounting bind: $target"
              umount "$target"
          fi

          if mountpoint -q "$staging" 2>/dev/null; then
              echo "  Unmounting RW NFS: $staging"
              umount "$staging"
          fi
      done

      echo "Unmounting RO root..."
      umount $SHADOW_ROOT

      echo "Shadow unmounted."

  - path: /usr/local/bin/shadow-sync
    permissions: '0755'
    content: |
      #!/bin/bash
      set -e

      # Host = the VM's default gateway (Multipass bridge address).
      HOST=$(ip route show default 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $3; exit}')
      PATHS_FILE=/etc/host-shadow-paths

      if [ -z "$HOST" ]; then
          echo "Could not determine host IP from default route." >&2
          exit 1
      fi

      echo "Syncing path list from host ($HOST)..."
      scp gherlein@$HOST:/etc/host-shadow-paths $PATHS_FILE
      echo "Done. Current RW paths:"
      grep -v '^\s*#' $PATHS_FILE | grep -v '^\s*$'

  # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  # RW path list — must match the host's /etc/host-shadow-paths exactly
  # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  - path: /etc/host-shadow-paths
    permissions: '0644'
    content: |
      # Canonical list of directories exported read-write from the host.
      # One absolute path per line. Blank lines and # comments are ignored.
      # Must match the host's /etc/host-shadow-paths exactly.
      /home/gherlein/h/src
      /home/gherlein/.claude
      /home/gherlein/.config

  # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Optional auto-mount on boot (fails silently if host NFS is unreachable)
  # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  - path: /etc/systemd/system/host-shadow.service
    permissions: '0644'
    content: |
      [Unit]
      Description=Host filesystem shadow mount
      After=network-online.target
      Wants=network-online.target

      [Service]
      Type=oneshot
      RemainAfterExit=yes
      ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/shadow-mount
      ExecStop=/usr/local/bin/shadow-umount

      [Install]
      WantedBy=multi-user.target

  # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Bash environment for the ubuntu user
  # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  - path: /home/ubuntu/.bashrc.d/dev-env.sh
    owner: ubuntu:ubuntu
    permissions: '0644'
    content: |
      # Go
      export GOROOT=/usr/local/go
      export GOPATH=$HOME/go
      export PATH="$GOROOT/bin:$GOPATH/bin:$PATH"

      # NVM lazy-load
      export NVM_DIR="$HOME/.nvm"
      nvm() { unset -f nvm node npm npx; . "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"; nvm "$@"; }
      node() { unset -f nvm node npm npx; . "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"; node "$@"; }
      npm()  { unset -f nvm node npm npx; . "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"; npm "$@"; }
      npx()  { unset -f nvm node npm npx; . "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"; npx "$@"; }
      # Make node available immediately without the lazy-load wrapper
      export PATH="$NVM_DIR/default/bin:$PATH" 2>/dev/null || true

      # npm global binaries
      export PATH="$HOME/.npm-global/bin:$PATH"

      # uv / Python local tools
      export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"

      # opencode
      export PATH="$HOME/.opencode/bin:$PATH"

      # Aliases
      alias emacs=mg
      alias clauded='claude --dangerously-skip-permissions'
      alias ll='ls -alF'
      alias gs='git status'
      alias gd='git diff'
      alias gl='git log --oneline -20'

runcmd:
  # --- Required directories -------------------------------------------------
  - mkdir -p /mnt/host
  - mkdir -p /mnt/rw
  - sudo -u ubuntu mkdir -p /home/ubuntu/go/bin /home/ubuntu/go/src /home/ubuntu/go/pkg
  - sudo -u ubuntu mkdir -p /home/ubuntu/.npm-global
  - sudo -u ubuntu mkdir -p /home/ubuntu/.bashrc.d
  - ln -sf /usr/bin/fdfind /usr/local/bin/fd

  # --- Source .bashrc.d from .bashrc ---------------------------------------
  - |
    cat >> /home/ubuntu/.bashrc << 'EOF'

    # Source all files in .bashrc.d
    if [ -d ~/.bashrc.d ]; then
      for f in ~/.bashrc.d/*.sh; do
        [ -r "$f" ] && . "$f"
      done
    fi
    EOF
    chown ubuntu:ubuntu /home/ubuntu/.bashrc

  # --- GitHub CLI -----------------------------------------------------------
  - |
    curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
      | dd of=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg
    chmod go+r /usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg
    echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list
    apt-get update
    apt-get install -y gh

  # --- Go 1.25.0 ------------------------------------------------------------
  - |
    GOARCH=$(dpkg --print-architecture)
    curl -fsSL "https://dl.google.com/go/go1.25.0.linux-${GOARCH}.tar.gz" \
      | tar --no-same-permissions --no-same-owner -xzC /usr/local

  # --- NVM + Node.js LTS ----------------------------------------------------
  - |
    NVM_DIR=/home/ubuntu/.nvm
    mkdir -p $NVM_DIR
    chown ubuntu:ubuntu $NVM_DIR
    sudo -u ubuntu bash -c '
      export NVM_DIR=/home/ubuntu/.nvm
      curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.39.0/install.sh | bash
      . "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"
      nvm install --lts
      nvm alias default node
      ln -s "$NVM_DIR/versions/node/$(. $NVM_DIR/nvm.sh && nvm version default)" "$NVM_DIR/default"
    '
    ln -sf $(sudo -u ubuntu bash -c '. /home/ubuntu/.nvm/nvm.sh && which node') /usr/local/bin/node
    ln -sf $(sudo -u ubuntu bash -c '. /home/ubuntu/.nvm/nvm.sh && which npm') /usr/local/bin/npm

  # --- npm global prefix + global packages ---------------------------------
  - sudo -u ubuntu npm config set prefix '/home/ubuntu/.npm-global'
  - |
    sudo -u ubuntu bash -c '
      export NVM_DIR=/home/ubuntu/.nvm
      . "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"
      NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=4096" npm install -g \
        typescript ts-node pnpm eslint prettier
      npm cache clean --force
    '

  # --- TinyGo 0.40.1 --------------------------------------------------------
  - |
    TINYGO_ARCH=$(dpkg --print-architecture)
    curl -fsSL "https://github.com/tinygo-org/tinygo/releases/download/v0.40.1/tinygo_0.40.1_${TINYGO_ARCH}.deb" -o /tmp/tinygo.deb
    dpkg -i /tmp/tinygo.deb
    rm /tmp/tinygo.deb

  # --- pi-go 0.0.32 ---------------------------------------------------------
  - |
    PI_GO_ARCH=$(dpkg --print-architecture)
    curl -fsSL "https://github.com/dimetron/pi-go/releases/download/v0.0.32/pi-go_0.0.32_linux_${PI_GO_ARCH}.tar.gz" -o /tmp/pi-go.tar.gz
    mkdir -p /tmp/pi-go-extract
    tar -xzf /tmp/pi-go.tar.gz -C /tmp/pi-go-extract
    mv /tmp/pi-go-extract/pi /usr/local/bin/pi
    chmod +x /usr/local/bin/pi
    rm -rf /tmp/pi-go.tar.gz /tmp/pi-go-extract

  # --- uv (Python package manager) -----------------------------------------
  - |
    sudo -u ubuntu bash -c '
      for i in 1 2 3; do
        curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh && break || \
          (echo "Attempt $i failed, retrying..." && sleep 5)
      done
    '

  # --- WeasyPrint via uv ----------------------------------------------------
  - sudo -u ubuntu /home/ubuntu/.local/bin/uv tool install weasyprint

  # --- Claude Code ----------------------------------------------------------
  - |
    sudo -u ubuntu bash -c '
      export PATH="/home/ubuntu/.npm-global/bin:$PATH"
      export NVM_DIR=/home/ubuntu/.nvm
      . "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"
      curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
    '

  # --- opencode -------------------------------------------------------------
  - sudo -u ubuntu bash -c 'curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | bash'

  # --- Go tools -------------------------------------------------------------
  - |
    sudo -u ubuntu bash -c '
      export GOROOT=/usr/local/go
      export GOPATH=/home/ubuntu/go
      export PATH="$GOROOT/bin:$GOPATH/bin:$PATH"
      go install golang.org/x/tools/cmd/goimports@latest
      go install github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/cmd/golangci-lint@latest
      go install github.com/go-delve/delve/cmd/dlv@latest
      go install github.com/mark3labs/kit/cmd/kit@latest
    '

  # --- Fix ownership --------------------------------------------------------
  - chown -R ubuntu:ubuntu /home/ubuntu

  # --- Enable optional auto-mount service ----------------------------------
  - systemctl daemon-reload
  - systemctl enable host-shadow.service

  # --- Completion marker ----------------------------------------------------
  - echo "devbox cloud-init complete" >> /var/log/cloud-init-custom.log

content.content: File created successfully at: /home/gherlein/dotfiles/goloo/.config/goloo/stacks/devbox/cloud-init.yaml (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

  - libusb-1.0-0-dev
  - usbutils
  - udev

  # Podman and rootless container support
  - podman
  - uidmap
  - fuse-overlayfs
  - slirp4netns

  # NFS client (for host shadow filesystem)
  - nfs-common

  # CA certificates and HTTPS tooling
  - ca-certificates
  - gnupg
  - lsb-release
  - software-properties-common
  - apt-transport-https

  # Search / navigation tools
  - ripgrep
  - fd-find
  - fzf

write_files:
  # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Podman rootless-friendly defaults (matches the Containerfile)
  # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  - path: /etc/containers/containers.conf
    permissions: '0644'
    content: |
      [containers]
      netns="host"
      userns="host"
      ipcns="host"
      utsns="host"
      cgroupns="host"
      cgroups="disabled"
      devices=["/dev/null"]

      [engine]
      cgroup_manager="cgroupfs"
      events_logger="file"
      runtime="crun"

  # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  # NFS shadow mount: RO root of host + RW overlays per listed path
  # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  - path: /usr/local/bin/shadow-mount
    permissions: '0755'
    content: |
      #!/bin/bash
      set -e

      # Host = the VM's default gateway (Multipass bridge address).
      HOST=$(ip route show default 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $3; exit}')
      SHADOW_ROOT=/mnt/host
      RW_STAGING=/mnt/rw
      PATHS_FILE=/etc/host-shadow-paths

      if [ -z "$HOST" ]; then
          echo "Could not determine host IP from default route." >&2
          exit 1
      fi

      if mountpoint -q $SHADOW_ROOT; then
          echo "Shadow already mounted at $SHADOW_ROOT"
          exit 0
      fi

      echo "Mounting RO shadow of host ($HOST) at $SHADOW_ROOT..."
      mount -t nfs4 -o ro,soft,timeo=30 $HOST:/ $SHADOW_ROOT

      while IFS= read -r path || [[ -n "$path" ]]; do
          [[ -z "$path" || "$path" =~ ^# ]] && continue

          staging=$RW_STAGING$path
          target=$SHADOW_ROOT$path

          echo "  Overlaying RW: $path"
          mkdir -p "$staging" "$target"
          mount -t nfs4 -o rw,soft,timeo=30 $HOST:$path $staging
          mount --bind $staging $target
      done < $PATHS_FILE

      echo "Shadow mounted. RO root at $SHADOW_ROOT, RW paths overlaid."

  - path: /usr/local/bin/shadow-umount
    permissions: '0755'
    content: |
      #!/bin/bash
      set -e

      SHADOW_ROOT=/mnt/host
      RW_STAGING=/mnt/rw
      PATHS_FILE=/etc/host-shadow-paths

      if ! mountpoint -q $SHADOW_ROOT; then
          echo "Shadow is not mounted."
          exit 0
      fi

      echo "Unmounting RW overlays..."

      # Reverse order so nested paths unmount before parents
      mapfile -t paths < <(grep -v '^\s*#' $PATHS_FILE | grep -v '^\s*$' | tac)

      for path in "${paths[@]}"; do
          target=$SHADOW_ROOT$path
          staging=$RW_STAGING$path

          if mountpoint -q "$target" 2>/dev/null; then
              echo "  Unmounting bind: $target"
              umount "$target"
          fi

          if mountpoint -q "$staging" 2>/dev/null; then
              echo "  Unmounting RW NFS: $staging"
              umount "$staging"
          fi
      done

      echo "Unmounting RO root..."
      umount $SHADOW_ROOT

      echo "Shadow unmounted."

  - path: /usr/local/bin/shadow-sync
    permissions: '0755'
    content: |
      #!/bin/bash
      set -e

      # Host = the VM's default gateway (Multipass bridge address).
      HOST=$(ip route show default 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $3; exit}')
      PATHS_FILE=/etc/host-shadow-paths

      if [ -z "$HOST" ]; then
          echo "Could not determine host IP from default route." >&2
          exit 1
      fi

      echo "Syncing path list from host ($HOST)..."
      scp gherlein@$HOST:/etc/host-shadow-paths $PATHS_FILE
      echo "Done. Current RW paths:"
      grep -v '^\s*#' $PATHS_FILE | grep -v '^\s*$'

  # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  # RW path list — must match the host's /etc/host-shadow-paths exactly
  # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  - path: /etc/host-shadow-paths
    permissions: '0644'
    content: |
      # Canonical list of directories exported read-write from the host.
      # One absolute path per line. Blank lines and # comments are ignored.
      # Must match the host's /etc/host-shadow-paths exactly.
      /home/gherlein/h/src
      /home/gherlein/.claude
      /home/gherlein/.config

  # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Optional auto-mount on boot (fails silently if host NFS is unreachable)
  # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  - path: /etc/systemd/system/host-shadow.service
    permissions: '0644'
    content: |
      [Unit]
      Description=Host filesystem shadow mount
      After=network-online.target
      Wants=network-online.target

      [Service]
      Type=oneshot
      RemainAfterExit=yes
      ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/shadow-mount
      ExecStop=/usr/local/bin/shadow-umount

      [Install]
      WantedBy=multi-user.target

  # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Bash environment for the ubuntu user
  # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
  - path: /home/ubuntu/.bashrc.d/dev-env.sh
    owner: ubuntu:ubuntu
    permissions: '0644'
    content: |
      # Go
      export GOROOT=/usr/local/go
      export GOPATH=$HOME/go
      export PATH="$GOROOT/bin:$GOPATH/bin:$PATH"

      # NVM lazy-load
      export NVM_DIR="$HOME/.nvm"
      nvm() { unset -f nvm node npm npx; . "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"; nvm "$@"; }
      node() { unset -f nvm node npm npx; . "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"; node "$@"; }
      npm()  { unset -f nvm node npm npx; . "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"; npm "$@"; }
      npx()  { unset -f nvm node npm npx; . "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"; npx "$@"; }
      # Make node available immediately without the lazy-load wrapper
      export PATH="$NVM_DIR/default/bin:$PATH" 2>/dev/null || true

      # npm global binaries
      export PATH="$HOME/.npm-global/bin:$PATH"

      # uv / Python local tools
      export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"

      # opencode
      export PATH="$HOME/.opencode/bin:$PATH"

      # Aliases
      alias emacs=mg
      alias clauded='claude --dangerously-skip-permissions'
      alias ll='ls -alF'
      alias gs='git status'
      alias gd='git diff'
      alias gl='git log --oneline -20'

runcmd:
  # --- Required directories -------------------------------------------------
  - mkdir -p /mnt/host
  - mkdir -p /mnt/rw
  - sudo -u ubuntu mkdir -p /home/ubuntu/go/bin /home/ubuntu/go/src /home/ubuntu/go/pkg
  - sudo -u ubuntu mkdir -p /home/ubuntu/.npm-global
  - sudo -u ubuntu mkdir -p /home/ubuntu/.bashrc.d
  - ln -sf /usr/bin/fdfind /usr/local/bin/fd

  # --- Source .bashrc.d from .bashrc ---------------------------------------
  - |
    cat >> /home/ubuntu/.bashrc << 'EOF'

    # Source all files in .bashrc.d
    if [ -d ~/.bashrc.d ]; then
      for f in ~/.bashrc.d/*.sh; do
        [ -r "$f" ] && . "$f"
      done
    fi
    EOF
    chown ubuntu:ubuntu /home/ubuntu/.bashrc

  # --- GitHub CLI -----------------------------------------------------------
  - |
    curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
      | dd of=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg
    chmod go+r /usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg
    echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list
    apt-get update
    apt-get install -y gh

  # --- Go 1.25.0 ------------------------------------------------------------
  - |
    GOARCH=$(dpkg --print-architecture)
    curl -fsSL "https://dl.google.com/go/go1.25.0.linux-${GOARCH}.tar.gz" \
      | tar --no-same-permissions --no-same-owner -xzC /usr/local

  # --- NVM + Node.js LTS ----------------------------------------------------
  - |
    NVM_DIR=/home/ubuntu/.nvm
    mkdir -p $NVM_DIR
    chown ubuntu:ubuntu $NVM_DIR
    sudo -u ubuntu bash -c '
      export NVM_DIR=/home/ubuntu/.nvm
      curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.39.0/install.sh | bash
      . "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"
      nvm install --lts
      nvm alias default node
      ln -s "$NVM_DIR/versions/node/$(. $NVM_DIR/nvm.sh && nvm version default)" "$NVM_DIR/default"
    '
    ln -sf $(sudo -u ubuntu bash -c '. /home/ubuntu/.nvm/nvm.sh && which node') /usr/local/bin/node
    ln -sf $(sudo -u ubuntu bash -c '. /home/ubuntu/.nvm/nvm.sh && which npm') /usr/local/bin/npm

  # --- npm global prefix + global packages ---------------------------------
  - sudo -u ubuntu npm config set prefix '/home/ubuntu/.npm-global'
  - |
    sudo -u ubuntu bash -c '
      export NVM_DIR=/home/ubuntu/.nvm
      . "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"
      NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=4096" npm install -g \
        typescript ts-node pnpm eslint prettier
      npm cache clean --force
    '

  # --- TinyGo 0.40.1 --------------------------------------------------------
  - |
    TINYGO_ARCH=$(dpkg --print-architecture)
    curl -fsSL "https://github.com/tinygo-org/tinygo/releases/download/v0.40.1/tinygo_0.40.1_${TINYGO_ARCH}.deb" -o /tmp/tinygo.deb
    dpkg -i /tmp/tinygo.deb
    rm /tmp/tinygo.deb

  # --- pi-go 0.0.32 ---------------------------------------------------------
  - |
    PI_GO_ARCH=$(dpkg --print-architecture)
    curl -fsSL "https://github.com/dimetron/pi-go/releases/download/v0.0.32/pi-go_0.0.32_linux_${PI_GO_ARCH}.tar.gz" -o /tmp/pi-go.tar.gz
    mkdir -p /tmp/pi-go-extract
    tar -xzf /tmp/pi-go.tar.gz -C /tmp/pi-go-extract
    mv /tmp/pi-go-extract/pi /usr/local/bin/pi
    chmod +x /usr/local/bin/pi
    rm -rf /tmp/pi-go.tar.gz /tmp/pi-go-extract

  # --- uv (Python package manager) -----------------------------------------
  - |
    sudo -u ubuntu bash -c '
      for i in 1 2 3; do
        curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh && break || \
          (echo "Attempt $i failed, retrying..." && sleep 5)
      done
    '

  # --- WeasyPrint via uv ----------------------------------------------------
  - sudo -u ubuntu /home/ubuntu/.local/bin/uv tool install weasyprint

  # --- Claude Code ----------------------------------------------------------
  - |
    sudo -u ubuntu bash -c '
      export PATH="/home/ubuntu/.npm-global/bin:$PATH"
      export NVM_DIR=/home/ubuntu/.nvm
      . "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"
      curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
    '

  # --- opencode -------------------------------------------------------------
  - sudo -u ubuntu bash -c 'curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | bash'

  # --- Go tools -------------------------------------------------------------
  - |
    sudo -u ubuntu bash -c '
      export GOROOT=/usr/local/go
      export GOPATH=/home/ubuntu/go
      export PATH="$GOROOT/bin:$GOPATH/bin:$PATH"
      go install golang.org/x/tools/cmd/goimports@latest
      go install github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/cmd/golangci-lint@latest
      go install github.com/go-delve/delve/cmd/dlv@latest
      go install github.com/mark3labs/kit/cmd/kit@latest
    '

  # --- Fix ownership --------------------------------------------------------
  - chown -R ubuntu:ubuntu /home/ubuntu

  # --- Enable optional auto-mount service ----------------------------------
  - systemctl daemon-reload
  - systemctl enable host-shadow.service

  # --- Completion marker ----------------------------------------------------
  - echo "devbox cloud-init complete" >> /var/log/cloud-init-custom.log

content.text:
Multipass isn't installed — and `snapd` (its normal install path) isn't present either. That's the last remaining blocker for `make create-vm`, and installing it is a significant host change I won't make without your say-so.

Here's where things stand.

## Fixed this session
- **`/etc/host-shadow-paths`** now lists three paths that actually exist (`/home/gherlein/h/src`, `/home/gherlein/.claude`, `/home/gherlein/.config`) → `host-setup` runs clean, NFS is exporting.
- **`setup-paths`** now always overwrites from the repo template (no more stale-defaults survival).
- **Makefile** stack name `localdev` → `devbox` everywhere, and `goloo delete` → `goloo destroy` (the actual command).
- **Created `config.json`** — schema verified against the goloo binary (`vm` section + `github_username`).
- **Created `cloud-init.yaml`** — full provisioning per REQUIREMENTS.md, with the NFS shadow scripts embedded. Both files validate (JSON + YAML parse clean; 7 `write_files`, 23 `runcmd` steps).

One adaptation worth knowing: the VM's shadow scripts derive the host IP from the VM's **default gateway at runtime** rather than the QEMU `10.0.2.2` default — Multipass on Linux uses its own bridge, so the hardcoded default in the docs would have been wrong.

## Two things to flag

**1. Multipass must be installed** before `make create-vm` works:
```
! sudo apt-get install -y snapd && sudo snap install multipass
```
Want me to add a `make install-multipass` target so this is captured in the stack?

**2. `/home/gherlein/.claude` is a symlink** → `/home/gherlein/dotfiles/bash/.claude`. `exportfs` followed it, so the host actually RW-exported the *target* path, not `/home/gherlein/.claude`. Inside the VM, the RW overlay for `.claude` will be **rejected** by the NFS server (only the real export point can be mounted RW). Options:
   - List `/home/gherlein/dotfiles/bash/.claude` instead of `/home/gherlein/.claude`, **or**
   - RW-export `/home/gherlein/dotfiles` (covers `.claude` and the rest of your dotfiles).

   I'd lean toward the second — want me to switch the path lists (host template + cloud-init) to `/home/gherlein/dotfiles`?

Separately, `README.md` still says `localdev` and shows the old `/localdev` + `/home/greg/projects` defaults — I can bring it in line with `devbox` and the real paths if you'd like. How do you want to handle the Multipass target, the `.claude` symlink, and the README?

content.content:
1	# localdev VM
2	
3	Disclaimer: This works for me — that's the entire guarantee. Built with AI in the loop, so check your own biases before you love it or hate it on principle. Use at your own risk, fork freely, and don't @ me when it explodes. (But do drop me a note if it helps — pay it forward.)
4	
5	---
6	
7	A local development VM provisioned by [goloo](https://github.com/emergingrobotics/goloo) using Multipass. It runs Ubuntu 24.04 and comes with the full dev toolchain pre-installed, plus an NFS shadow-mount system that makes the host filesystem visible inside the VM with selectable read-write paths.
8	
9	## What's inside the VM
10	
11	| Tool | Version |
12	|------|---------|
13	| Go | 1.25.0 |
14	| TinyGo | 0.40.1 |
15	| pi-go | 0.0.32 |
16	| Node.js | LTS (via nvm) |
17	| Python / uv | latest |
18	| Claude Code | latest |
19	| opencode | latest |
20	| GitHub CLI (`gh`) | latest |
21	| Podman | latest (rootless) |
22	| Go tools | goimports, golangci-lint, dlv, kit |
23	| npm globals | typescript, ts-node, pnpm, eslint, prettier |
24	| WeasyPrint | latest (via uv, for pandoc PDF output) |
25	| Editor | mg (aliased as `emacs`) |
26	
27	Plus the usual suspects: git, tmux, fzf, ripgrep, fd, jq, tree, direnv, zoxide, keychain, stow, ffmpeg, imagemagick, pandoc.
28	
29	## Host filesystem shadow mount
30	
31	The VM can mount a read-only shadow of the entire host filesystem at `/mnt/host`, with specific directories promoted to read-write. Writes on the RW paths go directly to the host. See [`VM-SHARED-MOUNTS.md`](VM-SHARED-MOUNTS.md) for the full design.
32	
33	Default RW paths (configured in `/etc/host-shadow-paths` on both host and VM):
34	
35	```
36	/localdev
37	/home/greg/projects
38	```
39	
40	### Usage inside the VM
41	
42	```bash
43	sudo shadow-mount          # mount: RO shadow at /mnt/host, RW overlays on top
44	ls /mnt/host/etc           # browse host filesystem read-only
45	cd /mnt/host/localdev/my-project && claude  # run Claude Code against a host project
46	sudo shadow-umount         # tear down all mounts cleanly
47	```
48	
49	The `host-shadow.service` systemd unit is enabled and will auto-mount on boot if the host NFS server is reachable.
50	
51	## Prerequisites
52	
53	- [goloo](https://github.com/emergingrobotics/goloo) installed and on PATH
54	- [Multipass](https://multipass.run/) installed
55	- SSH public keys on GitHub (used by goloo for passwordless VM access)
56	
57	## Quickstart
58	
59	### Step 1 — Set up the host (once)
60	
61	```bash
62	make host-setup

file.content:
# localdev VM

Disclaimer: This works for me — that's the entire guarantee. Built with AI in the loop, so check your own biases before you love it or hate it on principle. Use at your own risk, fork freely, and don't @ me when it explodes. (But do drop me a note if it helps — pay it forward.)

---

A local development VM provisioned by [goloo](https://github.com/emergingrobotics/goloo) using Multipass. It runs Ubuntu 24.04 and comes with the full dev toolchain pre-installed, plus an NFS shadow-mount system that makes the host filesystem visible inside the VM with selectable read-write paths.

## What's inside the VM

| Tool | Version |
|------|---------|
| Go | 1.25.0 |
| TinyGo | 0.40.1 |
| pi-go | 0.0.32 |
| Node.js | LTS (via nvm) |
| Python / uv | latest |
| Claude Code | latest |
| opencode | latest |
| GitHub CLI (`gh`) | latest |
| Podman | latest (rootless) |
| Go tools | goimports, golangci-lint, dlv, kit |
| npm globals | typescript, ts-node, pnpm, eslint, prettier |
| WeasyPrint | latest (via uv, for pandoc PDF output) |
| Editor | mg (aliased as `emacs`) |

Plus the usual suspects: git, tmux, fzf, ripgrep, fd, jq, tree, direnv, zoxide, keychain, stow, ffmpeg, imagemagick, pandoc.

## Host filesystem shadow mount

The VM can mount a read-only shadow of the entire host filesystem at `/mnt/host`, with specific directories promoted to read-write. Writes on the RW paths go directly to the host. See [`VM-SHARED-MOUNTS.md`](VM-SHARED-MOUNTS.md) for the full design.

Default RW paths (configured in `/etc/host-shadow-paths` on both host and VM):

```
/localdev
/home/greg/projects
```

### Usage inside the VM

```bash
sudo shadow-mount          # mount: RO shadow at /mnt/host, RW overlays on top
ls /mnt/host/etc           # browse host filesystem read-only
cd /mnt/host/localdev/my-project && claude  # run Claude Code against a host project
sudo shadow-umount         # tear down all mounts cleanly
```

The `host-shadow.service` systemd unit is enabled and will auto-mount on boot if the host NFS server is reachable.

## Prerequisites

- [goloo](https://github.com/emergingrobotics/goloo) installed and on PATH
- [Multipass](https://multipass.run/) installed
- SSH public keys on GitHub (used by goloo for passwordless VM access)

## Quickstart

### Step 1 — Set up the host (once)

```bash
make host-setup
```

This installs `nfs-kernel-server`, writes `/etc/host-shadow-paths`, installs the `shadow-export-gen` script, generates `/etc/exports`, and starts the NFS server.

If `ufw` is active on your host, also run:

```bash
make ufw-allow
```

### Step 2 — Create the VM

```bash
make create-vm
```

goloo fetches your SSH public keys from GitHub, injects them into the cloud-init template, and launches a Multipass VM. First boot takes 5–10 minutes while cloud-init installs everything.

### Step 3 — Connect

```bash
make ssh-vm
# or: goloo ssh localdev
```

## VM lifecycle

```bash
make create-vm     # goloo create localdev
make ssh-vm        # goloo ssh localdev
make status-vm     # goloo status localdev
make delete-vm     # goloo delete localdev
```

## Customising RW paths

To add or remove a read-write path:

**On the host:**

```bash
# Add a path
echo '/home/greg/new-thing' | sudo tee -a /etc/host-shadow-paths
sudo shadow-export-gen

# Remove a path: edit /etc/host-shadow-paths, then:
sudo shadow-export-gen
```

**In the VM:**

```bash
sudo shadow-sync              # pull updated list from host
sudo shadow-umount && sudo shadow-mount   # remount with new paths
```

## Dotfiles

If your dotfiles repo is structured for [stow](https://www.gnu.org/software/stow/) and lives at `/home/greg/dotfiles` on the host, add it to `config.json`:

```json
"mounts": [
  {"source": "/home/greg/dotfiles", "target": "/external/dotfiles"}
]
```

The VM's `.bashrc.d/dev-env.sh` automatically stows every package from `/external/dotfiles` (except `bash`, which it sources directly) on each shell login.

## Resizing

To change CPU, memory, or disk allocation, edit `~/.config/goloo/stacks/localdev/config.json`, then delete and recreate:

```bash
make delete-vm
make create-vm
```

## Repository layout

```
.
├── Makefile                    Host setup and VM lifecycle targets
├── README.md                   This file
├── REQUIREMENTS.md             Full specification for the VM and all its files
├── VM-SHARED-MOUNTS.md         NFS shadow-mount design and reference
├── example-Containerfile       Container equivalent (reference; not used by the VM)
└── host-setup/
    ├── shadow-export-gen       Script installed to /usr/local/bin on the host
    └── host-shadow-paths       Template for /etc/host-shadow-paths on the host

~/.config/goloo/stacks/localdev/
    ├── config.json             goloo VM spec (8 CPU / 8G / 80G / Ubuntu 24.04)
    └── cloud-init.yaml         Full provisioning script run on first boot
```

---

A local development VM provisioned by [goloo](https://github.com/emergingrobotics/goloo) using Multipass. It runs Ubuntu 24.04 and comes with the full dev toolchain pre-installed, plus an NFS shadow-mount system that makes the host filesystem visible inside the VM with selectable read-write paths.

## What's inside the VM

| Tool | Version |
|------|---------|
| Go | 1.25.0 |
| TinyGo | 0.40.1 |
| pi-go | 0.0.32 |
| Node.js | LTS (via nvm) |
| Python / uv | latest |
| Claude Code | latest |
| opencode | latest |
| GitHub CLI (`gh`) | latest |
| Podman | latest (rootless) |
| Go tools | goimports, golangci-lint, dlv, kit |
| npm globals | typescript, ts-node, pnpm, eslint, prettier |
| WeasyPrint | latest (via uv, for pandoc PDF output) |
| Editor | mg (aliased as `emacs`) |

Plus the usual suspects: git, tmux, fzf, ripgrep, fd, jq, tree, direnv, zoxide, keychain, stow, ffmpeg, imagemagick, pandoc.

## Host filesystem shadow mount

The VM can mount a read-only shadow of the entire host filesystem at `/mnt/host`, with specific directories promoted to read-write. Writes on the RW paths go directly to the host. See [`VM-SHARED-MOUNTS.md`](VM-SHARED-MOUNTS.md) for the full design.

Default RW paths (configured in `/etc/host-shadow-paths` on both host and VM):

```
/home/gherlein/h/src
/home/gherlein/dotfiles
/home/gherlein/.config
```

### Usage inside the VM

```bash
sudo shadow-mount          # mount: RO shadow at /mnt/host, RW overlays on top
ls /mnt/host/etc           # browse host filesystem read-only
cd /mnt/host/home/gherlein/h/src/my-project && claude  # run Claude Code against a host project
sudo shadow-umount         # tear down all mounts cleanly
```

The `host-shadow.service` systemd unit is enabled and will auto-mount on boot if the host NFS server is reachable.

## Prerequisites

- [goloo](https://github.com/emergingrobotics/goloo) installed and on PATH
- [Multipass](https://multipass.run/) installed
- SSH public keys on GitHub (used by goloo for passwordless VM access)

## Quickstart

### Step 1 — Set up the host (once)

```bash
make host-setup
```

This installs `nfs-kernel-server`, writes `/etc/host-shadow-paths`, installs the `shadow-export-gen` script, generates `/etc/exports`, and starts the NFS server.

If `ufw` is active on your host, also run:

```bash
make ufw-allow
```

### Step 2 — Create the VM

```bash
make create-vm
```

goloo fetches your SSH public keys from GitHub, injects them into the cloud-init template, and launches a Multipass VM. First boot takes 5–10 minutes while cloud-init installs everything.

### Step 3 — Connect

```bash
make ssh-vm
# or: goloo ssh localdev
```

## VM lifecycle

```bash
make create-vm     # goloo create localdev
make ssh-vm        # goloo ssh localdev
make status-vm     # goloo status localdev
make delete-vm     # goloo delete localdev
```

## Customising RW paths

To add or remove a read-write path:

**On the host:**

```bash
# Add a path
echo '/home/greg/new-thing' | sudo tee -a /etc/host-shadow-paths
sudo shadow-export-gen

# Remove a path: edit /etc/host-shadow-paths, then:
sudo shadow-export-gen
```

**In the VM:**

```bash
sudo shadow-sync              # pull updated list from host
sudo shadow-umount && sudo shadow-mount   # remount with new paths
```

## Dotfiles

If your dotfiles repo is structured for [stow](https://www.gnu.org/software/stow/) and lives at `/home/greg/dotfiles` on the host, add it to `config.json`:

```json
"mounts": [
  {"source": "/home/greg/dotfiles", "target": "/external/dotfiles"}
]
```

The VM's `.bashrc.d/dev-env.sh` automatically stows every package from `/external/dotfiles` (except `bash`, which it sources directly) on each shell login.

## Resizing

To change CPU, memory, or disk allocation, edit `~/.config/goloo/stacks/localdev/config.json`, then delete and recreate:

```bash
make delete-vm
make create-vm
```

## Repository layout

```
.
├── Makefile                    Host setup and VM lifecycle targets
├── README.md                   This file
├── REQUIREMENTS.md             Full specification for the VM and all its files
├── VM-SHARED-MOUNTS.md         NFS shadow-mount design and reference
├── example-Containerfile       Container equivalent (reference; not used by the VM)
└── host-setup/
    ├── shadow-export-gen       Script installed to /usr/local/bin on the host
    └── host-shadow-paths       Template for /etc/host-shadow-paths on the host

~/.config/goloo/stacks/localdev/
    ├── config.json             goloo VM spec (8 CPU / 8G / 80G / Ubuntu 24.04)
    └── cloud-init.yaml         Full provisioning script run on first boot
```

- SSH public keys on GitHub (used by goloo for passwordless VM access)

A local development VM provisioned by [goloo](https://github.com/emergingrobotics/goloo) using Multipass. It runs Ubuntu 24.04 and comes with the full dev toolchain pre-installed, plus an NFS shadow-mount system that makes the host filesystem visible inside the VM with selectable read-write paths.

## What's inside the VM

| Tool | Version |
|------|---------|
| Go | 1.25.0 |
| TinyGo | 0.40.1 |
| pi-go | 0.0.32 |
| Node.js | LTS (via nvm) |
| Python / uv | latest |
| Claude Code | latest |
| opencode | latest |
| GitHub CLI (`gh`) | latest |
| Podman | latest (rootless) |
| Go tools | goimports, golangci-lint, dlv, kit |
| npm globals | typescript, ts-node, pnpm, eslint, prettier |
| WeasyPrint | latest (via uv, for pandoc PDF output) |
| Editor | mg (aliased as `emacs`) |

Plus the usual suspects: git, tmux, fzf, ripgrep, fd, jq, tree, direnv, zoxide, keychain, stow, ffmpeg, imagemagick, pandoc.

## Host filesystem shadow mount

The VM can mount a read-only shadow of the entire host filesystem at `/mnt/host`, with specific directories promoted to read-write. Writes on the RW paths go directly to the host. See [`VM-SHARED-MOUNTS.md`](VM-SHARED-MOUNTS.md) for the full design.

Default RW paths (configured in `/etc/host-shadow-paths` on both host and VM):

```
/home/gherlein/h/src
/home/gherlein/dotfiles
/home/gherlein/.config
```

### Usage inside the VM

```bash
sudo shadow-mount          # mount: RO shadow at /mnt/host, RW overlays on top
ls /mnt/host/etc           # browse host filesystem read-only
cd /mnt/host/home/gherlein/h/src/my-project && claude  # run Claude Code against a host project
sudo shadow-umount         # tear down all mounts cleanly
```

The `host-shadow.service` systemd unit is enabled and will auto-mount on boot if the host NFS server is reachable.

## Prerequisites

- [goloo](https://github.com/emergingrobotics/goloo) installed and on PATH
- [Multipass](https://multipass.run/) installed (`make install-multipass`, or run as part of `make host-setup`)
- SSH public keys on GitHub (used by goloo for passwordless VM access)

## Quickstart

### Step 1 — Set up the host (once)

```bash
make host-setup
```

This installs Multipass (via snap), installs `nfs-kernel-server`, writes `/etc/host-shadow-paths`, installs the `shadow-export-gen` script, generates `/etc/exports`, and starts the NFS server.

If `ufw` is active on your host, also run:

```bash
make ufw-allow
```

### Step 2 — Create the VM

```bash
make create-vm
```

goloo fetches your SSH public keys from GitHub, injects them into the cloud-init template, and launches a Multipass VM. First boot takes 5–10 minutes while cloud-init installs everything.

### Step 3 — Connect

```bash
make ssh-vm
# or: goloo ssh devbox
```

## VM lifecycle

```bash
make create-vm     # goloo create devbox
make ssh-vm        # goloo ssh devbox
make status-vm     # goloo status devbox
make delete-vm     # goloo destroy devbox
```

## Customising RW paths

To add or remove a read-write path:

**On the host:**

```bash
# Add a path
echo '/home/gherlein/new-thing' | sudo tee -a /etc/host-shadow-paths
sudo shadow-export-gen

# Remove a path: edit /etc/host-shadow-paths, then:
sudo shadow-export-gen
```

**In the VM:**

```bash
sudo shadow-sync              # pull updated list from host
sudo shadow-umount && sudo shadow-mount   # remount with new paths
```

## Dotfiles

If your dotfiles repo is structured for [stow](https://www.gnu.org/software/stow/) and lives at `/home/greg/dotfiles` on the host, add it to `config.json`:

```json
"mounts": [
  {"source": "/home/greg/dotfiles", "target": "/external/dotfiles"}
]
```

The VM's `.bashrc.d/dev-env.sh` automatically stows every package from `/external/dotfiles` (except `bash`, which it sources directly) on each shell login.

## Resizing

To change CPU, memory, or disk allocation, edit `~/.config/goloo/stacks/localdev/config.json`, then delete and recreate:

```bash
make delete-vm
make create-vm
```

## Repository layout

```
.
├── Makefile                    Host setup and VM lifecycle targets
├── README.md                   This file
├── REQUIREMENTS.md             Full specification for the VM and all its files
├── VM-SHARED-MOUNTS.md         NFS shadow-mount design and reference
├── example-Containerfile       Container equivalent (reference; not used by the VM)
└── host-setup/
    ├── shadow-export-gen       Script installed to /usr/local/bin on the host
    └── host-shadow-paths       Template for /etc/host-shadow-paths on the host

~/.config/goloo/stacks/localdev/
    ├── config.json             goloo VM spec (8 CPU / 8G / 80G / Ubuntu 24.04)
    └── cloud-init.yaml         Full provisioning script run on first boot
```

content.text:
All done — everything's fixed and validated. No `localdev`/`greg` references remain in the README or Makefile, the path list is updated, and both generated files parse cleanly.

## Summary of changes

**Multipass install**
- Added `make install-multipass` (checks if already present; else installs `snapd` + `multipass` via snap).
- Wired it into the `make host-setup` chain, and documented it in the Makefile help + README prerequisites.

**`.claude` symlink fix**
- Replaced `/home/gherlein/.claude` with `/home/gherlein/dotfiles` in the path list (repo template + `cloud-init.yaml`). Since `.claude` → `dotfiles/bash/.claude` is a **relative** symlink, it now resolves correctly to the RW-exported `dotfiles` tree inside the shadow mount (and this also covers `.config/goloo`, another relative symlink into dotfiles). Final RW set: `h/src`, `dotfiles`, `.config`.

**README**
- `localdev` → `devbox` throughout; `goloo delete` → `goloo destroy`; default RW paths and example paths updated to the real `gherlein` paths; Multipass install step added; dotfiles section rewritten (your dotfiles are now reachable RW at `/mnt/host/home/gherlein/dotfiles` via the shadow).

One thing I left alone on purpose: `REQUIREMENTS.md` and `VM-SHARED-MOUNTS.md` still use the original `localdev`/`greg`/`/localdev` examples. Those are spec/reference docs describing the original design; say the word if you want them brought in line too.

## To finish the setup

The installed `/etc/host-shadow-paths` still has the old `.claude` entry — re-run host-setup to push the new list and (now) install Multipass:

```
! make host-setup
```

Then:
```
! make create-vm
```

`host-setup` now installs Multipass, overwrites `/etc/host-shadow-paths` with the three corrected paths, and regenerates the exports; `create-vm` will find the new `config.json` + `cloud-init.yaml`.

That's the right command — `snap install multipass` pulls the snap and sets up the `mpqemubr0` bridge, which can take a minute or two. (It's also what `make install-multipass` would have run.)

Once it finishes, verify and continue:

```

! make create-vm
```

content.text:
`snap` itself isn't installed (that's why `snapd` was absent earlier). Install snapd first, then multipass:

```
! sudo apt-get install -y snapd
! sudo snap install multipass
```

A couple of notes:
- After installing `snapd`, it may need a few seconds to seed before `snap install` works — if the multipass install errors with something about snapd not ready, wait ~10s and retry.
- If `snap` isn't found on PATH right after install, it's at `/usr/bin/snap`; a fresh shell (or `hash -r`) picks it up.

This is exactly the sequence `make install-multipass` runs, so alternatively just:

```
! make install-multipass
```

content.text:
Found it. Snap is **deliberately blocked** on this host — there's a pin file that makes `snapd` uninstallable:

```
/etc/apt/preferences.d/nosnap.pref
  Package: snapd
  Pin: release a=*
  Pin-Priority: -1
```

The package exists in the repos (snapd 2.75.2 is right there in `noble-updates/main`), but priority `-1` tells apt "never install this." This is the well-known "de-snap" pref — someone (or the base image) intentionally disabled snap on this machine.

That matters because **Multipass on Linux is distributed only as a snap** — there's no apt package, and goloo's local provider uses Multipass. So to create the VM, snap has to be re-enabled. Your call, since you (or your image) deliberately turned it off:

```
! sudo rm /etc/apt/preferences.d/nosnap.pref
! sudo apt-get update
! sudo apt-get install -y snapd
! sudo snap install multipass
```

Removing the pin just *allows* snapd to be installed; it doesn't force anything else. If you keep the pin around for a reason (e.g. to stop other packages pulling in snap), you could move it aside instead of deleting (`sudo mv .../nosnap.pref /tmp/`) and restore it later — though leaving snapd installed is what Multipass needs ongoing.

Want me to make `make install-multipass` detect this pin and print a clear message (rather than the cryptic "no installation candidate") so the stack is self-explanatory next time? I'd have it *warn and stop*, not auto-delete a system policy file.

content: Goal: get the goloo `devbox` VM creatable. All config files, Makefile, and docs are fixed; the only blocker is that snap is pinned off, and Multipass needs it. Next: run the four commands to remove the pin and install snapd plus multipass. (disable recaps in /config)

message.content: no, I don't want snap on my hosts.  so I will likely pivot though I do hate to give up the name.  So, give me 10 names for a similar project that we will build around libvirt + virt-install + cloud-init

content.text:
No snap — fair, and libvirt + virt-install + cloud-init is a cleaner, daemon-native path for exactly this. A nice bonus: libvirt literally calls VMs **domains**, so there's rich wordplay to mine. Ten names, with how they'd read as a CLI:

1. **demesne** — a feudal estate you hold *directly*; libvirt VMs are literally "domains." Elegant, on-theme. `demesne up devbox`
2. **domu** — Xen's term for a guest domain (`domU`). Short, techy, unmistakably virtualization. `domu create devbox`
3. **hearth** — the home fire; your dev environment's base camp on the host. Warm, memorable. `hearth up`
4. **kiln** — fires raw clay (your cloud-init spec) into a finished machine. Short, evocative, easy to type. `kiln fire devbox`
5. **forge** — provision a machine from raw spec; pairs naturally with "build." (Common, but strong.) `forge create devbox`
6. **burrow** — dig into an isolated little environment on your host. Cozy, unix-y. `burrow dig devbox`
7. **warren** — a network of burrows = a fleet of local VMs. Good if you'll run several. `warren up devbox`
8. **virtwright** — one who builds virt machines (cf. wheelwright/playwright); descriptive and unusual. `virtwright build devbox`
9. **kindle** — kindle a VM from a spec; quick, light. (Mild Amazon overlap.) `kindle devbox`
10. **sprout** — spin a fresh environment fast from a seed (cloud-init = seed config). `sprout devbox`

If you want to keep the *feel* of `devbox` while changing the project name, **hearth**, **kiln**, and **demesne** are my top three — short, distinctive, and none collide with existing big tools (unlike forge/anvil/nimbus). And you don't necessarily have to give up "devbox" at all — it can stay the **default stack/VM name** under whichever project name you pick (`hearth create devbox`).

Want me to scaffold the libvirt version? The `cloud-init.yaml` carries over almost verbatim; the main swaps are `goloo create` → a `virt-install --cloud-init` invocation, and the NFS host-IP detection moves to the libvirt default network gateway (`192.168.122.1` on the standard `virbr0`), which is actually more stable than the Multipass bridge was.

content.text:
Good instinct — the project's in Go, so the name should wink at it. Here are ten that bake "go" in, several carried over from the previous concepts:

1. **golem** — "go" sits right inside it, and the metaphor is *exact*: you animate raw clay (a cloud-init spec) into a living machine. (← kiln) `golem create devbox`
2. **forgo** — forge + go, and it's a real word so it reads naturally. Forge a VM from raw spec. (← forge) `forgo up devbox`
3. **govirt** — go + `virt-install`; the honest, descriptive one. Libvirt-adjacent, trivial to type. `govirt up devbox`
4. **godom** / **godomain** — go + libvirt's actual unit, the *domain*. Technically on-the-nose. (← demesne/domu) `godom create devbox`
5. **vertigo** — "virt" + "go" hidden in a real word. Punchy and memorable. `vertigo up devbox`
6. **goburrow** — go + burrow; dig an isolated little env on the host. (← burrow) `goburrow dig devbox`
7. **godomus** — go + Latin *domus* (home/domain) — ties libvirt "domains" to "home base." (← hearth/demesne) `godomus up`
8. **golab** — go + lab; a local lab of throwaway VMs. Neutral, clean. `golab up devbox`
9. **dragoon** — "drago" carries the "go"; dragon = fire-breathing (kiln imagery), and "dragoon into service" fits spinning up workers. `dragoon devbox`
10. **amigo** — ends in "go"; the friendly local helper that gets your dev box running. `amigo up devbox`

My top three: **golem** (best fit — "go" embedded *and* the provisioning metaphor is perfect), **forgo** (clever, it's a real word), and **govirt** (clearest about what it does). As before, `devbox` can stay the default stack name under any of them — `golem create devbox`.

Want me to scaffold the libvirt/virt-install version under one of these? The `cloud-init.yaml` carries over almost verbatim; the swaps are `goloo create` → `virt-install --cloud-init`, and the NFS host-IP detection moves to libvirt's stable `virbr0` gateway (`192.168.122.1`) instead of the Multipass bridge.

## Naming Conflict Research Results

**1. godom — MEDIUM — Multiple active DOM parsing/manipulation Go libraries with same/similar names; unrelated to devops/VM space — siongui/godom, richi0/goDOM, mlavergn/godom (web-focused)**

**2. godomain — LOW — Niche security tools exist (goDomain for LDAP enumeration); no VM provisioning or CLI tool ecosystem collision — Rvn0xsy/goDomain (Active Directory pentesting)**

**4. vertigo — HIGH — Registered trademark (Vertigo, LLC; Serial #97000764 for software/SAAS services); major established company (founded 1997, Microsoft Partner); multiple active GitHub projects (vmarmol/vertigo, vertigo-io org, vertigo-web org); trademark and brand confusion risk across devops space — Vertigo Software Inc., Vertigo, LLC (trademark), vmarmol/vertigo (Docker scaling), vertigo-io/vertigo-core (Java framework)**

---

- **godom**: Most problematic for a CLI tool—multiple active web-oriented projects with identical/similar names
- **godomain**: Safe—existing projects are security-focused, not VM/infrastructure
- **goburrow**: Safe—established org with distinct utility focus, no infrastructure collision
- **vertigo**: Legally risky—registered trademark + major software company + ecosystem saturation

For each name below, search the web and report: (1) any well-known open-source project, software product, company, or registered trademark using that exact name, (2) how severe the collision is for a Go-based devops/VM CLI tool specifically (high/medium/low), and (3) whether the name appears taken on GitHub as a notable repo/org or as a package.

Names to check:
1. "godomus" — also search "domus home automation", "godomus github"
2. "golab" — also search "golab conference", "golab.io", "golab go"
3. "dragoon" — also search "dragoon software", "dragoon github", "dragoon game trademark"
4. "amigo" — also search "amigo software", "amigo ontology", "amigo go", "amigo trademark"

Use WebSearch (and WebFetch if needed). Be concise. Return a short structured verdict per name: NAME — severity (high/medium/low) — one-line reason — notable existing user(s). Do not recommend; just report facts.

content.text:
Based on my research, here is the structured verdict for each candidate name:

---

**1. godomus** — LOW — Existing Go package for Lifedomus home automation client (niche domain, unmaintained) — github.com/sgrimee/godomus (minor collision)

**2. golab** — MEDIUM — Well-established annual Go conference (golab.io, founded 2015, held in Florence) + multiple Go packages already registered in pkg.go.dev (gophergala/golab, cloudingcity/golab, dimkatsi91/golab) — High potential for user confusion in Go ecosystem

**3. dragoon** — HIGH — "The Legend of Dragoon" is an active registered trademark owned by Sony Interactive Entertainment LLC (USPTO #75838812) covering video game software. Additionally, multiple dragoon Go packages exist (luizbranco/dragoon, osamingo/dragoon) and the Legend of Dragoon modding community is active on GitHub — Trademark holder is major corporation

**4. amigo** — MEDIUM-HIGH — Multiple active trademark holders: Vivint Amigo Inc. (mobile app software), PLATINUM PMO LLC (enterprise data migration software), AmiGo Travel Inc. All have registered trademarks. Additionally, highly used name across GitHub with 9+ active Go packages (ivahaev/amigo, vvksh/amigo, alexisvisco/amigo, austinmajor/amiGO, etc.), plus AmiGO (Gene Ontology) is a well-known bioinformatics project — High collision risk with existing commercial/research products and packages

---

**Summary:** Dragoon carries trademark risk from Sony. Amigo has multiple trademark and software conflicts. Golab conflicts with an active conference brand and multiple packages. Godomus is the safest but already taken as a minor package name.

Sources:
- [godomus Go package](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/sgrimee/godomus)
- [GoLab Conference](https://golab.io/)
- [golab gophergala](https://github.com/gophergala/golab)
- [The Legend of Dragoon Trademark - Sony](https://uspto.report/TM/75838812)
- [Dragoon Go packages](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/luizbranco/dragoon)
- [Vivint Amigo Trademark](https://trademarks.justia.com/884/02/amigo-88402970.html)
- [PLATINUM PMO AMIGO Trademark](https://trademarks.justia.com/860/24/amigo-86024562.html)
- [AmiGO Gene Ontology](https://github.com/geneontology/amigo)
- [amigo ivahaev Go package](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/ivahaev/amigo)

lastPrompt: check those for conflicts with existing open source projects or trademarks/existing products
