content.text:
Research is back. Here's the conflict assessment, sorted cleanest-first:

| Name | Risk | Why |
|------|------|-----|
| **govirt** | 🟢 Low | Only collision is `lino/govirt`, a dormant 5-star libvirt wrapper. No trademark. Bonus: literally describes the tool. |
| **goburrow** | 🟢 Low | `goburrow` GitHub org exists (modbus/serial/logging) but zero overlap with VM/devops; won't be confused. |
| **godomain** | 🟢 Low | Only a niche AD-pentest tool (`Rvn0xsy/goDomain`). No product/trademark in this space. |
| **godomus** | 🟢 Low | One unmaintained home-automation client (`sgrimee/godomus`). Obscure. |
| **forgo** | 🟡 Medium | `forgojs/forgo` (1.2k-star JS UI runtime) + **forego** (Go Procfile tool, phonetically identical). Search-confusion in dev tooling. |
| **godom** | 🟡 Medium | Several active Go **DOM** libraries (`siongui/godom` et al.) — same ecosystem, different domain. Muddy. |
| **golab** | 🟡 Medium | **GoLab.io** — established annual Go conference (Florence, since 2015) + several Go packages. Brand clash inside the Go world. |
| **amigo** | 🟠 High | Multiple registered trademarks (Vivint, Platinum PMO), `AmiGO` Gene Ontology, 9+ Go packages. Crowded. |
| **golem** | 🔴 High | **Registered trademark** (Golem Factory GmbH, USPTO #79222597) + major crypto/compute network (11k+ stars). Avoid. |
| **vertigo** | 🔴 High | **Registered trademark** (Vertigo LLC) + established software company + multiple `vertigo` GitHub orgs. Avoid. |
| **dragoon** | 🔴 High | **Sony trademark** "The Legend of Dragoon" (USPTO #75838812) + Go packages. Avoid. |

**Bottom line:** my earlier favorites — **golem** and **vertigo** — are both hard no's (live trademarks). Of the names that survive, the cleanest and most fitting is **govirt** (low conflict *and* self-describing: go + virt-install). If you want something less literal, **goburrow** is the next-safest with personality.

Two cheap follow-ups worth doing before you commit to one: check that the GitHub org name and the Go module path (`github.com/<you>/govirt`) are free. Want me to verify `govirt` (and a backup) are available as a GitHub org + that the name's clear on pkg.go.dev?

content.text:
Verified. Results:

**GitHub:** `github.com/govirt` — **free** (404 on both the page and the API). The org name is available to claim.

**pkg.go.dev:** Go has no central name registry — module identity is the full repo path, so claiming the `govirt` org gives you a clean `github.com/govirt/govirt`. That said, a few unrelated `*/govirt` repos exist under *other* users' paths, all dormant hobby/example projects with **0 imports**:
- `github.com/intrand/govirt` — oVirt example wrapper (2022)
- `github.com/wvcardoso/govirt/cmd` — (2022)
- `github.com/Hari-Kiri/govirt-qemu-connector` — 1 import
- `github.com/lino/govirt` — exists on GitHub but **not even indexed** on pkg.go.dev (404)

**Bottom line:** `govirt` is clear to use.
- ✅ GitHub org `govirt` available
- ✅ No trademark (from earlier research)
- ✅ Module path `github.com/govirt/govirt` would be unique and clean
- ⚠️ Minor: a handful of abandoned, zero-import `*/govirt` repos exist, so the *bare word* isn't globally unique — but none are notable and none block you. If you want maximum SEO/discoverability distinctiveness, that's the only caveat.

Want me to scaffold the project under `govirt` (libvirt + virt-install + cloud-init), carrying over the `cloud-init.yaml` and adapting the Makefile/host-IP logic off the Multipass assumptions?

message.content: why make it github.com/govirt instead of under emergingrobotics

aiTitle: Debug NFS export path failures in devbox setup

content.text:
`emergingrobotics/govirt` is free, and the org already hosts `goloo`. Honestly there's no reason to make a dedicated `govirt` org — I just reached for it reflexively. Putting it under `emergingrobotics` is the better call:

**Why `emergingrobotics/govirt` wins**
- **It's a sibling to goloo.** This is conceptually the libvirt successor to your Multipass-based goloo tooling — same family, same maintainer, discoverable together.
- **Module path stays clean:** `github.com/emergingrobotics/govirt` is just as unique and tidy as a dedicated org would be. Go doesn't care which org owns it.
- **Zero overhead.** No new org to create, manage members/settings/billing for, or keep alive.
- **Established trust.** A repo under an existing org with history reads as more legitimate than a brand-new single-repo org.

**The only reasons you'd want a dedicated `govirt` org instead** — and none seem to apply here:
- You expect `govirt` to grow into a *suite* of repos that deserve their own namespace/branding.
- You want the vanity bare-name URL `github.com/govirt` for marketing.
- You want to hand it off / govern it separately from your other work someday.

So: **`github.com/emergingrobotics/govirt`**. The bare-word caveat from before (dormant `*/govirt` repos) is irrelevant here since your path is namespaced by the org anyway.

Want me to scaffold it there — port the `cloud-init.yaml`, swap `goloo create` → `virt-install --cloud-init`, and rework the host-IP logic off Multipass onto libvirt's `virbr0` (`192.168.122.1`)?

lastPrompt: why make it github.com/govirt instead of under emergingrobotics
